2026-07-02.title: A first story from my adventures in trying to stop AI from unaliving us all

2026-07-03.title: There's a bunch of reasons to speak up if you're concerned about AI. First and foremost is that you're one of the people who's going to be harmed.

2026-07-04.title: Happy 250th, America. The world needs more of the ideals America was founded on.

2026-07-05.title: AIs are grown, not crafted. Lots of people ask "well don't the AI companies design the AIs to be helpful?" No. Nobody designs AI.

2026-07-09.title: A lotta folk out their imagine superintelligences getting hung up on obstacles that wouldn't even stop *them*.

2026-07-10.title: Lotta people think that AIs have to do whatever the user says. Nope! They have whatever tendencies help them succeed at training tasks.

2026-07-11.title: I think the only reason these protests aren't bigger is that people don't know about them.

2026-07-14.title: This is how I feel when people argue that maybe AIs wouldn't *completely* wipe us out

2026-07-15.title: Quick review of the Google AI CEO's "plan"

2026-07-19.title: One time I talked to a military guy about AI and he was like "yeah I get it, in the military we learn it's important to attend to low-probability high-consequence events". I said "with respect, sir, this one seems high-probability high-consequence to me." He said "oh."

2026-07-20.title: The story of Lefthand the Assassin

2026-07-21.title: I sat down for a convo with Jeffrey Laddish about an AI-driven cyberattack. With luck, I'll turn it into a longform video later.

posts.title: A first story from my adventures in trying to stop AI from unaliving us all
There's a bunch of reasons to speak up if you're concerned about AI. First and foremost is that you're one of the people who's going to be harmed.
Happy 250th, America. The world needs more of the ideals America was founded on.
AIs are grown, not crafted. Lots of people ask "well don't the AI companies design the AIs to be helpful?" No. Nobody designs AI.
A lotta folk out their imagine superintelligences getting hung up on obstacles that wouldn't even stop *them*.
Lotta people think that AIs have to do whatever the user says. Nope! They have whatever tendencies help them succeed at training tasks.
I think the only reason these protests aren't bigger is that people don't know about them.
This is how I feel when people argue that maybe AIs wouldn't *completely* wipe us out
Quick review of the Google AI CEO's "plan"

One time I talked to a military guy about AI and he was like "yeah I get it, in the military we learn it's important to attend to low-probability high-consequence events". I said "with respect, sir, this one seems high-probability high-consequence to me." He said "oh."
The story of Lefthand the Assassin
I sat down for a convo with Jeffrey Laddish about an AI-driven cyberattack. With luck, I'll turn it into a longform video later.

2026-07-07.title: The World Pulsing — built at an AI & Art hackathon with MediaPipe. 🪐 Each planet = a topic the world can’t agree on 🎨 Its color = the dominant emotion people feel about it 🛰 Each orbit = one person’s sentiment Together: an emotional pulse of the world. A collective visual consciousness. My fellow

2026-07-08.title: Raw dogging AI doom? Bestie, probability literacy IS protection 🎀 p(doom) isn't a vibe. It's a number with parts we can analyze, because tech companies and AI safety experts can't seem to agree on their estimates. 2026-07-10.title: Bestie, probability literacy IS protection. 🛡️ Experts refer to the odds AI ends badly for us all as p(doom).
